title: Do You Need a Realtor for New Construction?
content:
Why having your own real estate agent when buying new construction is strongly recommended.

KEY POINT: Builder representatives work for the builder, not you. Their contracts are designed to protect the builder.

5 BENEFITS OF YOUR OWN AGENT:
1. Full Support: Agent serves as project manager, upgrade strategist, and contract negotiator from builder selection through closing
2. Asking the Right Questions: Experienced agents identify critical issues like utility placement, quality control, soil testing, grading requirements
3. Smarter Upgrade Decisions: Prioritize structural upgrades (higher ceilings, larger windows, extra garage bays) before cosmetic enhancements
4. Negotiation: While builders rarely reduce base prices, agents can negotiate upgrade credits, closing incentives, favorable terms
5. Problem Resolution: When construction delays or installation errors occur, your agent advocates for corrections or compensatory upgrades

FINANCIAL REALITY: You won't save money without an agent. Builder commissions are already factored into pricing — declining representation means the builder keeps that money.

QUESTIONS TO ASK POTENTIAL REALTORS:
- How many new construction clients have they represented?
- Builder relationships?
- How do they handle delays or defects?
- Can they explain upgrade value and resale implications?
- Will they attend walkthroughs and construction meetings?
